twitter_posts.title:
This model is insane at design.

I asked GLM 5.2 (left) and Opus 4.8 (right) to build me a landing page and you can't even tell the difference.

GLM cost $0.06 while opus cost $0.49. More than 6x chea
twitter_posts.description:
This model is insane at design.

I asked GLM 5.2 (left) and Opus 4.8 (right) to build me a landing page and you can't even tell the difference.

GLM cost $0.06 while opus cost $0.49. More than 6x cheaper while being faster + more token efficient.

Another win for open source AI.
twitter_posts.author: Hassan El Mghari

twitter_posts.title:
R to @AnthropicAI: Domain experts—as judged by the questions they ask and vocabulary they use about a subject—are more likely to see success.

But the gap between intermediate and expert users is quit
twitter_posts.description:
Domain experts—as judged by the questions they ask and vocabulary they use about a subject—are more likely to see success.

But the gap between intermediate and expert users is quite modest, suggesting that proficiency in a domain is sufficient to code successfully within it.
twitter_posts.author: Anthropic

twitter_posts.title:
R to @AnthropicAI: We compared Claude Code success rates between occupations. 

On our toughest measure of success—requiring verifiable evidence that a goal was completed, like committed code—every fi
twitter_posts.description:
We compared Claude Code success rates between occupations. 

On our toughest measure of success—requiring verifiable evidence that a goal was completed, like committed code—every field was within 7 percentage points of software engineering.
